title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Detective Superintendent Spence discuss two investigative leads regarding the murder of Mrs. McKinty. Poirot decides his first course of action will be to meet with James Bentley, after which he intends to travel to Broadchurch to investigate the "Mrs. McKinty line" to eliminate it as a possibility. Although Spence suggests Poirot use a false identity, such as a singer with a damaged voice, Poirot insists on traveling under his true identity to exert influence and observe reactions, as he remains unsatisfied with the verdict in the Bentley case.

Poirot arrives at the Long Grass Hotel, a dilapidated country courtyard, where he finds the accommodations intolerable due to dust, broken furniture, a mangy dog, and a broken door latch that allows cold wind to swirl through the room. While there, he interacts with the hostess, Molly Samuels, who is busy managing the household and dealing with hens that have entered the larder. During their conversation, Samuels mentions that her previous maid, Miss MacKnight, was murdered, which caused a local scandal; she describes MacKnight as a reliable but gossiping woman. When Poirot asks about Mrs. McKinty, Samuels remarks that Mrs. McKinty's life of scrubbing floors seemed terrible. The interaction is interrupted when Colonel Samoys appears at the window, causing Samuels to jump and scatter beans across the floor.
